items.source: SF Competitor field vs. Avoma calls naming Conga/Coupa/DocuSign

items.quote: A director-level stakeholder referenced across multiple 2025 calls requested the gap analysis but could not be matched to a verified Salesforce contact.
items.source: Avoma Zycus Debrief, 16 Oct 2025; no matching SF contact

items.quote: ARUP finally released their RFP for CLM

sources: Avoma CLM Demo Test run with Jordan, 9 Sep 2025
metrics.narrative: Michael Tutton described a specific, painful version-control failure at ARUP where a CFO-level stakeholder banned redlining in Salesforce after simultaneous reviewers overwrote each other's comments during a major sourcing event (CLM Demo Debrief, 3 Sep 2025). Pain is also described qualitatively around manual authoring, disconnected systems (Salesforce for redlining, Monday for tracking), and lack of obligation/risk tracking, but no dollar figure, cycle-time number, or headcount impact has been quantified anywhere in the record. Quantifying the cost of these manual failures (hours lost, contract-cycle delay) should be a named move ahead of the 10 Jul proposal.

sources: Avoma Call with Michael Tutton, 4 Feb 2026
champion.narrative: Michael Tutton is a strong, longtime champion: he referenced sourcing Zycus originally roughly nine years ago and has personally driven this deal since May 2025, absorbing a botched initial demo, rebuilding it with a Zycus specialist, and coordinating a coalition with Ondraya Watkins and Robert Stipanovich. His advocacy peaked in mid-2026 when he 'convinced management to kick off an RFP for CLM' with Zycus explicitly included (SF Next_Step__c, 1 Jun and 29 Jun 2026) after describing internal replacement of Conga/Salesforce as 'a hard sell.' He remains actively engaged, replying with the released RFP as recently as 24-29 Jun 2026. No sign of cooling; the open question is whether his internal influence extends to the still-unverified director who holds final sign-off.

sources: Avoma Catch up with Zycus, 9 Jan 2026
Avoma Call with Michael Tutton, 4 Feb 2026
competition.narrative: The primary competitive threat is not an external vendor but ARUP's own incumbent Conga (on Salesforce): the buying team ran three rounds of internal gap-analysis meetings comparing Conga's repository/redlining/reporting against Zycus (Catch up with Zycus, 9 Jan 2026; Call with Michael Tutton, 4 Feb 2026), and the director sponsoring that comparison explicitly asked 'what can we change in Salesforce Conga... and how does that compare to the Zycus tool.' Coupa and DocuSign were mentioned only as feature-comparison points by the Zycus rep during early demos (2 Sep 2025) and are not buyer-driven threats. The newly released RFP (29 Jun 2026) confirms ARUP is running a multi-vendor process, implying unnamed co-bidders whose identities are not yet known. Zycus's wedge is CLM depth Conga structurally lacks: AI-driven redlining, risk scoring, clause library, external-counsel WordConnect editing, and D365-integrated obligation tracking.

sources: Avoma Zycus X ARUP - Demo Prep Session, 22 Aug 2025
Avoma Zycus CLM Demo Debrief, 3 Sep 2025
Avoma ARUP Labs X Zycus, 6 May 2025
identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed pain: entirely manual contract authoring/redlining via Word and Salesforce with no centralized tracking (Demo Prep Session, 22 Aug 2025); a version-control breakdown severe enough that a CFO-level stakeholder banned Salesforce redlining outright (CLM Demo Debrief, 3 Sep 2025); disconnected systems (Salesforce, Conga, Monday) causing blind spots on obligations and milestones (multiple calls, Aug-Oct 2025); and a recent D365 implementation that disrupted procurement workflows (ARUP Labs X Zycus, 6 May 2025). Pain owners are Michael Tutton and Ondraya Watkins, who own the contracting workflow day to day.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, or procurement-channel discussion has occurred; the deal is still in the RFP-response phase. The RFP explicitly reserves an IT security questionnaire and system evaluation for shortlisted vendors after proposals are submitted (10 Jul 2026), which has not yet started. Visibility on contracting mechanics will only emerge once ARUP names its shortlist; that should be asked for directly in the proposal cover conversation.

sources: Avoma Zycus X ARUP Labs - Overview + CLM Demo, 2 Sep 2025
economic_buyer.narrative: No Salesforce-verified economic buyer exists on this opportunity. Calls repeatedly reference a director-level stakeholder above Arthur Moes who requested the formal gap analysis between Conga and Zycus and who was 'a little confused' and skeptical after the first demo (Zycus X ARUP Labs - Overview + CLM Demo, 2 Sep 2025; Zycus Debrief, 16 Oct 2025). This person's identity could not be matched to any Salesforce contact, so no name or title can be asserted; treat this role as 'the ARUP director evaluating build-vs-buy' pending verification. Budget sign-off authority therefore remains unconfirmed even as the RFP moves forward.

sources: Avoma Catch up with Zycus, 9 Jan 2026
Avoma Call with Michael Tutton, 4 Feb 2026
decision_process.narrative: The process ran: initial demo (6 Aug 2025) → a failed broad demo that lost the room (2 Sep 2025, self-described as 'a failure' by Michael Tutton) → a scripted CLM-only re-demo with a Zycus specialist (13 Oct 2025) → internal gap-analysis meetings pitting Zycus against an enhanced Conga build (three rounds referenced 9 Jan 2026) → a mid-February 2026 decision checkpoint to pick a direction or issue an RFP/RFI (2 Feb 2026 call) → formal RFP release (29 Jun 2026) with Intent to Respond, Questions, and Proposal deadlines. The approval chain runs from the sourcing team (Tutton, Watkins, Stipanovich) up through the unverified director, with IT security review reserved for shortlisted vendors post-proposal.

sources: Avoma Zycus X ARUP - CLM Demo, 13 Oct 2025
decision_criteria.narrative: Across three demo cycles (Aug-Oct 2025) the buying team converged on concrete evaluation criteria: contract redlining and version control depth, a clause library, external/outside-counsel document collaboration (WordConnect), AI-driven risk scoring, D365 ERP integration for contract utilization, and post-contract obligation dashboards. The now-released RFP formalizes this into technical, functional, and commercial requirements due 10 Jul 2026, with granular per-user and per-envelope pricing required across iContract, Certinal, and the supplier-risk module.

engagement.top_event: [Clari - Email Sent] Re: Zycus: RFP – Consolidated Responses to Submitted Questions

items.name: ARUP sends consolidated RFP Q&A to all suppliers
items.summary: Mzia Sturua (ARUP IT PMO) distributed a consolidated document of all questions and answers submitted by RFP participants, formally incorporating them into the RFP documentation and inviting further questions via reply.

items.name: Steve acknowledges receipt
items.summary: Steve Ovadje replied with a brief thank-you confirming receipt of the consolidated Q&A doc — no further questions raised, closing the loop on this exchange for now.

day_summary.overall: ARUP's PMO sent all RFP participants a consolidated Q&A document to keep the competitive process fair and transparent, formally rolling those clarifications into the RFP documentation. Steve simply acknowledged receipt, so the ball is now in ARUP's court to run the rest of the RFP timeline — no open asks on our side beyond monitoring for further updates.

cro_panel.intro: Zycus is the RFP-included, deeply demoed CLM front-runner thanks to a multi-year internal champion, but the parallel Conga build-vs-buy case and unfinalized per-user pricing are the only things standing between Zycus and a win

blocks.read: We're in it, with a slight edge.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Champion is driving it — Michael Tutton has driven this deal single-handedly for over a year, absorbing a failed demo, rebuilding it, and personally winning management approval to launch the RFP with Zycus in scope.

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — The live threat is internal, not external: ARUP's own Salesforce/Conga team is pitching an in-house enhancement path and has run three rounds of gap-analysis meetings with the buying team as recently as early 2026. Conga (incumbent, on Salesforce) is therefore ranked the strongest current threat given its renewal-style default advantage and the director's explicit request to compare 'what can we change in Salesforce Conga' against Zycus. The newly released RFP (29 Jun 2026) confirms a multi-vendor process with unnamed co-bidders whose identities are unknown. Coupa and DocuSign surfaced only as minor, Zycus-side comparison points during 2025 demos and show no buyer-driven interest.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 24 is driven by Close-date risk recurred across 2 sweeps. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 68 is carried by momentum 85 vs stage-expected 56; champion strength +1.00 (weight 15) — from Next-Step/narrative: 'champion' in Next-Step/narrative. Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 85 (moving forward): engagement points 35.0 on the RFP process clock (month-old deliverable work keeps weight). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 40 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 2 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 57 is anchored on the stage win (68), nudged by commitment 40 and momentum 85, and dragged by risk 24. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: champion strength +1.00 (weight 15) — from Next-Step/narrative: 'champion' in Next-Step/narrative

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AIS field value is populated in Salesforce. Calls show genuine AI interest (inventory-replenishment automation, negotiation agent, risk scoring) but recurring confusion about Merlin's data sourcing that was never cleared up after the 3 Sep 2025 demo debrief.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain (version-control failure, manual redlining) is well articulated but never quantified in dollars or cycle time.

deal_movement.summary: The deal spent most of 2025-2026 as an internal championing effort inside ARUP's sourcing team; the decisive movement is the formal RFP release on 29 Jun 2026, which converts a year of demos and internal debate into a dated, competitive process.

items.detail: CloseDate has been pushed at least 12 times since the original 2025-12-31 target, most recently to 2026-07-31, which is not credible against the RFP's own post-proposal evaluation steps.
No confirmed economic buyer / final decision-maker in Salesforce; calls point to an unverified director-level stakeholder whose identity could not be matched to a Salesforce contact.
ARUP's RFP demands granular per-user and per-envelope pricing across iContract, Certinal, and supplier risk modules ahead of the 2026-07-10 deadline; commercial proposal not yet finalized.
ARUP's RFP explicitly reserves an IT security questionnaire / IT system evaluation for shortlisted vendors post-proposal; not yet started.
Champion expects internally replacing the Conga/Salesforce incumbent to be 'a hard sell.'
Per-user/per-envelope pricing across iContract, Certinal, and supplier risk not yet finalized; a March 2026 discount offer went unanswered.
The director-level stakeholder weighing Conga vs. Zycus remains unverified in Salesforce and was described as skeptical.
Close date of 31 Jul 2026 cannot accommodate the RFP's own proposal deadline (10 Jul) plus post-proposal evaluation.
RFP reserves an IT security questionnaire/system evaluation for shortlisted vendors; not yet started.
ARUP's own Salesforce/Conga team is running a parallel in-house enhancement case, creating a build-vs-buy political risk even if Zycus wins technically.

critical_signals.text: The newly released RFP demands granular per-user and per-envelope pricing across iContract, Certinal, and the supplier-risk module by 10 Jul 2026, and that pricing is not yet finalized.
A discounted pricing offer made in March 2026 went unanswered, and the buyer still hasn't confirmed the user counts and envelope volumes needed to price the RFP response.
The real fight is internal: ARUP's own Salesforce and Conga team is building a parallel case for enhancing their existing tool instead of buying Zycus.
The champion's year-long push finally got management to formally launch the RFP with Zycus guaranteed a seat, the biggest forward step since the relationship began.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live: a genuine two-way buyer touch 13 days ago and a major, dated milestone (RFP release) 8 days ago outweigh the long internal-only stretch through 2025.

north_star_verdict.math: Opportunity created 2025-07-25 (~347 days open as of 2026-07-07). Close date 2026-07-31 is only 24 days away, but the RFP proposal Zycus must submit isn't due until 2026-07-10 (~3 days from today), and the RFP itself reserves a further IT security questionnaire and evaluation step for shortlisted vendors AFTER proposals are in. None of that fits inside the remaining 21 days after submission, so the close date will slip regardless of how the RFP result lands.
evidence: SF Next_Step__c updated 2026-06-29: RFP released with Intent to Respond 6/9, Questions 6/16, Proposal due 7/10/2026
SF CloseDate 2026-07-31 vs proposal deadline 2026-07-10 leaves no time for post-proposal evaluation
north_star_verdict.headline: Michael Tutton's year-long internal campaign finally landed a formal RFP with Zycus guaranteed a seat, but the 31 Jul 2026 close date cannot survive a process whose own proposal deadline (10 Jul 2026) hasn't been met yet.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (push close date to Q4 2026 at the earliest, pending RFP scoring, security review, and contracting)

flags: With 'several participants' confirmed in ARUP's live RFP (24 Jun 2026), differentiate on AI-assisted simultaneous review and redlining (Merlin) rather than price, since Conga's core documented gap is manual, non-simultaneous review.

Equip champion Michael Tutton with a quantified ROI/savings case before the 10 Jul 2026 proposal, since he has already flagged expecting 'a hard sell' internally and currently has only qualitative pain points to argue with.

No economic buyer or final decision-maker is confirmed in Salesforce this late in the cycle; use the RFP's own promised post-proposal 'demonstrations, reference checks' phase to get in front of the real approver and name them in Salesforce.

ARUP's own Conga/Salesforce team is pitching an in-house enhancement path in parallel to the RFP; the proposal must quantify what Conga structurally cannot do (redlining, risk scoring, D365-integrated obligation reporting), not rely on live demos alone.
No Salesforce-verified economic buyer sits above the sourcing team; push for a review with the director evaluating build-vs-buy before the proposal lands so the decision isn't made on price alone.

Reinforce Zycus's Gartner Magic Quadrant placement (shared with Michael Tutton in Jan 2026) directly inside the RFP response, since it was explicitly flagged as a credibility lever for Michael's internal sell.
best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy levers to pull before the 10 Jul 2026 proposal lands.

customer_preference.evidence: Zycus offers enhanced functionality such as integrated risk scoring and easier workflows (Michael Tutton, 16 Oct 2025); champion secured Zycus's inclusion in the RFP.

competitive_position.summary: The live threat is internal, not external: ARUP's own Salesforce/Conga team is pitching an in-house enhancement path and has run three rounds of gap-analysis meetings with the buying team as recently as early 2026. Conga (incumbent, on Salesforce) is therefore ranked the strongest current threat given its renewal-style default advantage and the director's explicit request to compare 'what can we change in Salesforce Conga' against Zycus. The newly released RFP (29 Jun 2026) confirms a multi-vendor process with unnamed co-bidders whose identities are unknown. Coupa and DocuSign surfaced only as minor, Zycus-side comparison points during 2025 demos and show no buyer-driven interest.

competitors.name: Conga (incumbent, on Salesforce)
competitors.quote: What can we change in Salesforce Conga or enhance or whatever to get to where we feel like we need to be, and how does that compare to the Zycus tool?
competitors.source: Avoma Catch up with Zycus, 9 Jan 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Lead the RFP response with what Conga structurally cannot do: AI risk scoring, clause library, external-counsel WordConnect editing, and D365-integrated obligation dashboards.

competitors.name: Unnamed RFP co-bidders
competitors.quote: ARUP finally released their RFP for CLM with the following due dates.
competitors.how_we_win: Ask Michael Tutton directly for the vendor field once shortlisting occurs so the wedge can be tailored.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Coupa was mentioned as a competitor; however, it lacks the robust contract management functionality offered by Zycus.
competitors.source: Avoma Zycus CLM Demo Debrief, 3 Sep 2025
competitors.how_we_win: Not buyer-driven; no action needed unless it resurfaces.

competitors.name: DocuSign (e-signature only)
competitors.quote: Do you use Docusign, or do you have your own signing module?
competitors.source: Avoma Zycus X ARUP - Initial Demo, 6 Aug 2025
competitors.how_we_win: Positioned as a feature comparison point (Certinal), not a standalone competitive threat.

champion_strength.evidence: Michael Tutton got Zycus written into the RFP after a year-plus campaign (Next Step, 1 Jun 2026).

economic_buyer_engagement.evidence: Director-level stakeholder unverified and skeptical, with no direct outreach since 16 Oct 2025.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is the RFP-included, deeply demoed CLM front-runner thanks to a multi-year internal champion, but the parallel Conga build-vs-buy case and unfinalized per-user pricing are the only things standing between Zycus and a win.

deal_risk.text: The 31 Jul 2026 close date sits only three weeks after the 10 Jul 2026 RFP proposal deadline, leaving no realistic time for scoring, the reserved IT security questionnaire, and contracting.
No Salesforce-verified economic buyer exists above the sourcing-manager level, so final budget authority for the CLM purchase is still unconfirmed.

win_position.text: Michael Tutton spent over a year building an internal coalition and personally convinced ARUP management to launch the CLM RFP with Zycus guaranteed a seat (Next Step, 1 Jun and 29 Jun 2026).

ARUP's own Salesforce/Conga team is running a parallel gap-analysis case for an in-house enhancement, and the director evaluating both paths remains unverified in Salesforce and was described as skeptical as of 16 Oct 2025.

Zycus has not finalized per-user and per-envelope pricing across iContract, Certinal, and the supplier-risk module, and ARUP has not confirmed the user counts and envelope volumes needed to price it before the 10 Jul 2026 deadline.

deal_momentum.text: The RFP for CLM was formally released on 29 Jun 2026, the most concrete forward step since discovery began in May 2025.
A March 2026 discount offer went unanswered and the close date has been pushed repeatedly, most recently to 31 Jul 2026, a date the newly-released RFP timeline cannot support.

customer_commitment.text: Michael Tutton has stayed engaged for over a year, coordinated multiple demo redos, built a buying coalition with Ondraya Watkins and Robert Stipanovich, and replied to Zycus again on 24 Jun 2026 with the released RFP.
Arthur Moes and the unverified director-level stakeholder remained skeptical as of 16 Oct 2025 and have not re-engaged directly with Zycus since.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI/Merlin generated real curiosity in demos (agentic negotiation, risk scoring) but also confusion about how Merlin builds its knowledge base versus ChatGPT/Copilot, which was never fully resolved.

items.quote: Zycus offers enhanced functionality such as integrated risk scoring and easier workflows.
items.criterion: Contract redlining/version control depth beyond Conga's repository

items.quote: That is a game changer for our legal team.
items.source: Avoma ARUP Discussion with Zycus, 8 Sep 2025

items.quote: There is confusion regarding how Merlin gathers data and builds its knowledge base, especially in contrast to ChatGPT and Copilot.
items.source: Avoma Zycus CLM Demo Debrief, 3 Sep 2025

items.quote: How many times do I get nickeled and dimed as I kind of build out this suite of things?

customer_expectations_fit.summary: ARUP's evaluation team sees clear differentiation in Zycus's CLM depth and external-collaboration features, but Merlin/AI clarity and modular pricing transparency remain exposed.

hard.ais_why:
• On the 92-minute 9/2/2025 workshop, the only AI-tagged commitment is Aakriti Srivastava sending Michael Tutton's team a screenshot of the AI capability for identifying risky clauses in contracts. That is a buyer-pulled AI evaluation, but it sits as one item inside a 12-item action list dominated by supplier onboarding, contract approval flow, SAP adapter integration, FedRAMP, and sub-tier mapping.
• Michael Tutton's own action items are organisational, not technical: lead internal discussions on ARUP's current ERP system capabilities, identify CLM gaps, coordinate with other ARUP stakeholders, and evaluate the need for further demos. He is sizing the CLM scope across ARUP rather than pulling on AI. No second AI-specific buyer ask surfaced across the action set or filtered transcript keyword review.
• AI is flagged Important at the account level and Merlin and Merlin Intake are in product scope. The risky-clause ask plus active engagement keeps this above AI Resistant, but no POC, no AI-tied budget, no named AI champion, and a buyer agenda still focused on CLM gap analysis means it stays at the lower end of AI Curious.
hard.next_step:
6/29- ARUP finally released their RFP for CLM with the following due dates.


  • Intent to Respond Due: 06/09/2026
  • RFP Questions Due: 06/16/2026
  • Proposal Submission Deadline: 07/10/2026



6/1 - No material Update, Michaels efforts are finally paying off. He has convinced management to kick off an RFP for CLM ands Zycus will be included. He will share Project timeline and next steps once final approval has been granted.


5/26 - Michaels efforts are finally paying off. He has convinced management to kick off an RFP for CLM ands Zycus will be included. He will share Project timeline and next steps once final approval has been granted.


5/23 - No Material update


5/13 Michael is working hard on our behalf and trying to bring in stakeholders


3/30 - Michael confirmed they are not in a place to sign up today (end of march) but soon he will reach out soon to schedule the demo with additional stakeholders.


3/12 - Offered potential March Discount. No response yet.


/18 They are finalizing the appication rationalization and moving towards a zycus solution. We will need to demo the ELT as final step

2/10 Confirmed attendance for Procurement Pinnacle Feb 18


2/2 - Decision target by mid-February to choose direction or issue (our) RFP/RFI. IN the meantime, I sent Gartner Report + Information on Procurecon west to support their internal discussions with leadership. Aligned for March timeline

value.name: Conga (incumbent, on Salesforce)
value.quote: What can we change in Salesforce Conga or enhance or whatever to get to where we feel like we need to be, and how does that compare to the Zycus tool?
value.source: Avoma Catch up with Zycus, 9 Jan 2026
value.how_we_win: Lead the RFP response with what Conga structurally cannot do: AI risk scoring, clause library, external-counsel WordConnect editing, and D365-integrated obligation dashboards.

value.name: Conga (incumbent, on Salesforce)
value.quote: the biggest gap we have with Salesforce is simultaneous review
value.source: Avoma call 2025-09-03 "Zycus CLM Demo Debrief"; reinforced by 2026-02-04 call ("expects a hard sell internally")
value.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin AI-assisted simultaneous review and redlining directly against Conga's documented gap; make it the proposal's headline differentiator.

packets.subject: Conga (incumbent, on Salesforce)

value.name: Unnamed RFP co-bidders
value.quote: ARUP finally released their RFP for CLM with the following due dates.
value.how_we_win: Ask Michael Tutton directly for the vendor field once shortlisting occurs so the wedge can be tailored.

packets.subject: Unnamed RFP co-bidders

value.name: Coupa
value.quote: Coupa was mentioned as a competitor; however, it lacks the robust contract management functionality offered by Zycus.
value.source: Avoma Zycus CLM Demo Debrief, 3 Sep 2025
value.how_we_win: Not buyer-driven; no action needed unless it resurfaces.

value.name: Coupa
value.quote: Coupa was mentioned as a competitor; however, it lacks robust contract management functionality.
value.how_we_win: No action needed; Tutton's own past experience with Coupa already favors Zycus on CLM depth.

packets.subject: Coupa

value.name: DocuSign (e-signature only)
value.quote: Do you use Docusign, or do you have your own signing module?
value.source: Avoma Zycus X ARUP - Initial Demo, 6 Aug 2025
value.how_we_win: Positioned as a feature comparison point (Certinal), not a standalone competitive threat.

value.name: DocuSign (e-signature only)
value.quote: For e-signature, is embedded signing acceptable, or must the solution integrate with a specific provider (e.g., DocuSign, Adobe Sign)?
value.how_we_win: Confirm Certinal's embedded-signing flexibility satisfies this requirement without needing a DocuSign integration.

packets.subject: DocuSign (e-signature only)
